title: Fix handling of remove of bogus volumes, networks and Pods
body:
In podman containers rm and podman images rm, the commands
exit with error code 1 if the object does not exists.

This PR implements similar functionality to volumes, networks, and Pods.

Similarly if volumes or Networks are in use by other containers, and return
exit code 2.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

You will also need to `s/125/1/` here https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/84dede4452d4f85ad6dacf2c66e00500685c24af/test/system/500-networking.bats#L102

but hold off until CI finishes, there may be more that I didn't catch

@Luap99 I think we should look at that secondarily.  To make sure that they work consistently.

I am not sure of the correct way to handle if multiple objects are being removed and they have different errors.  Which exit code wins.  Although I think this is a corner case.

So close!
```
podman-remote [options] network rm bogus
Error: json: cannot unmarshal object into Go struct field NetworkRmReport.Err of type error
```
@edsantiago Well at least that was an unfound error in the API.

@vrothberg @giuseppe @samc24 @Luap99 This one is ready to go in. PTAL
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL
